text.title: AI Agent Patterns — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ai-agent-patterns` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Pattern picked along two axes — loop shape (linear / iterative / tree) and multi-agent need (single / sequential / parallel / supervisor / hierarchical).
rule.rationale: Single-axis picks miss the multi-agent question; two-axis picks force the right framing.

rule.statement: Default to ReAct + structured tools unless the task explicitly needs planning, reflection, or branching.
rule.rationale: Most agent tasks fit ReAct; defaulting saves debate cycles and reduces over-engineering.

rule.statement: If loop depth ≥ 5 and steps are predictable, use Plan-Execute. ReAct loops > 5 steps degrade interpretability.
rule.rationale: Long ReAct loops accumulate context noise; planning ahead reduces backtracking.

rule.statement: When output quality depends on self-correction (writing, code review), add a Reflection step.
rule.rationale: Reflection adds ~30% latency but reduces error rate ~40% on subjective-quality tasks.

rule.statement: Multi-agent only when distinct roles add value (researcher + writer + reviewer). Multi-agent for breadth alone is rejected.
rule.rationale: Multi-agent for breadth doubles cost without quality gain; for distinct roles it justifies the overhead.
